cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Andrew could have gone to the coffee shop. The question gives us a timeline of events and when Andrew was seen doing certain things. Let me break this down step by step. First, Andrew woke up at 8am. So he's definitely up by then. Then, the next thing we know is that Elizabeth saw him fixing his computer at the electronic store from 11am to 3pm. So that's a four-hour window where he was at the store. Then Jennifer saw him at the bike shop from 3pm to 4pm buying a bike. Then Anthony saw him at the bakery from 4pm to 6pm buying cookies. And finally, Betty saw him at the theater watching a movie from 6pm to 8pm. Also, the coffee shop was closed after 8pm, so he had to go before that. So we need to find the time when Andrew wasn't doing any of these activities and the coffee shop was open. Let's map out his day: - 8am: Wakes up. - 11am to 3pm: Electronic store. - 3pm to 4pm: Bike shop. - 4pm to 6pm: Bakery. - 6pm to 8pm: Theater. - Coffee shop closed after 8pm. So between waking up at 8am and being at the electronic store at 11am, there's a window from 8am to 11am. Then after that, he's continuously occupied until 8pm. The coffee shop is open until 8pm, so if he went during the time before his first scheduled activity (the electronic store at 11am), that would be possible. Let's check the options: Option C is 8am to 11am. That seems like the only time he's not accounted for. Let me make sure there's no other time. From 11am to 3pm, he's at the store.
